solutions.title: ADAS Camera Network Solution
solutions.name: ADAS Camera Network Solution
solutions.shortDescription: Complete camera connectivity solution for ADAS applications featuring high-speed SerDes and automotive Ethernet for reliable video transmission.
solutions.description: Comprehensive camera network solution utilizing Jisemi's SerDes and automotive Ethernet PHYs for high-resolution camera connectivity in ADAS systems.
solutions.longDescription:
The ADAS Camera Network Solution from jisemi represents a comprehensive approach to addressing modern challenges in Industrial. This solution integrates advanced semiconductor technology with robust design methodologies to deliver exceptional performance and reliability.

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of jisemi components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. The solution supports wide operating ranges and maintains performance across varying environmental conditions. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

Implementation is streamlined through comprehensive design support including reference designs, simulation models, and detailed application notes. BeiLuo's FAE team provides expert guidance throughout the development cycle from concept through production. Customization options are available to address specific application requirements.

The solution has been validated through extensive testing including functional verification, environmental stress screening, and long-term reliability assessment. Field deployment data demonstrates consistent performance and high customer satisfaction across diverse applications and operating conditions.
benefits: High-bandwidth video transmission supporting up to 4K resolution cameras
Power-over-coax eliminates separate power wiring to camera modules
AEC-Q100 qualified components ensure automotive reliability
ASIL-B functional safety support for safety-critical applications
Scalable architecture from 4 to 16+ camera systems
solutions.coreAdvantage: Jisemi's ADAS camera solution delivers superior video quality and system reliability through high-performance SerDes and automotive Ethernet components designed specifically for demanding automotive camera applications. The integrated power delivery and diagnostics reduce system complexity while ensuring robust operation.
coreAdvantages: High-performance SerDes enabling 4K video transmission over cost-effective coaxial cable
Integrated power-over-coax simplifies camera module design and reduces wiring
AEC-Q100 Grade 1 qualification ensures reliable operation in harsh automotive environments
ASIL-B functional safety support meets requirements for safety-critical ADAS applications
Comprehensive diagnostic capabilities enable remote monitoring and predictive maintenance

products.name: 2Gbps Camera SerDes
products.role: Video transmission from camera modules to processor

results: Passed AEC-Q100 qualification and EMC testing
customerCase.testimonial: The Jisemi camera solution exceeded our performance expectations. The power-over-coax feature significantly simplified our camera module design, and the video quality is excellent even with long cable runs.

faeInsight.summary: ADAS camera network design requires careful attention to signal integrity, power delivery, and EMC performance. Key success factors include proper cable selection, PCB layout, and shielding.
faeInsight.decisionLogic: Consider camera resolution, cable length, and power requirements when selecting SerDes devices. Higher resolution cameras require higher bandwidth SerDes.
faeInsight.solutionFramework: Start with reference design, optimize for specific camera configuration, validate with comprehensive testing including EMC and environmental.
keyPoints: Use quality coaxial cable for best signal integrity and power delivery
Implement proper grounding and shielding for EMC compliance
Consider cable length when selecting equalization settings
Validate thermal performance under worst-case conditions
commonIssues: Inadequate cable quality affecting signal integrity
Insufficient power delivery to camera modules
EMC issues due to poor shielding or grounding
Thermal management in high-density camera systems
bestPractices: Use Jisemi evaluation boards for initial validation
Implement comprehensive production testing including cable diagnostics
Plan for future expansion in camera count

faeInsights.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Camera Systems
faeInsights.content: Based on extensive field experience with ADAS camera systems, successful implementation requires careful attention to cable quality, grounding, and signal integrity. The Jisemi SerDes devices provide excellent performance margin, but proper PCB layout and cable selection are critical. I always recommend using quality coaxial cable with proper shielding termination. For power-over-coax applications, verify voltage drop calculations under maximum load conditions. The diagnostic features in Jisemi devices are invaluable for production testing and field troubleshooting - use them to verify cable integrity and identify connectivity issues early. Thermal management is often overlooked but critical for long-term reliability, especially in high-density camera modules.
keyTakeaways: Cable quality directly impacts video performance and reliability
Proper grounding and shielding are essential for EMC compliance
Use diagnostic features for production testing and troubleshooting
Verify thermal design under worst-case operating conditions
keyPoints: Use quality coaxial cable with proper shielding
Verify power delivery voltage drop calculations
Test EMC performance early in design cycle
commonIssues: Poor cable quality causing signal degradation
Insufficient power delivery to remote cameras
Thermal issues in compact camera modules
bestPractices: Use Jisemi reference designs as starting point
Implement cable diagnostics in production test
Plan thermal management for worst-case conditions

steps: Define camera resolution and count requirements
Select appropriate SerDes based on bandwidth

faqs.question: What is the maximum cable length for camera connectivity?
faqs.answer: Jisemi SerDes solutions support up to 15 meters of coaxial cable at maximum data rates. The actual maximum length depends on cable quality, with high-quality cables enabling longer reach. For applications requiring longer distances, consider using signal conditioning or selecting higher-performance SerDes devices with advanced equalization.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use high-quality coaxial cable for maximum reach; contact FAE for specific cable recommendations.

faqs.question: How much power can be delivered over coaxial cable?
faqs.answer: Power-over-coax capability depends on cable resistance and voltage drop requirements. Typical implementations deliver 5-12W to camera modules over 10-15m cables. Higher power requires thicker cable or shorter distances. Calculate voltage drop based on cable AWG, length, and camera power consumption to ensure adequate voltage at the camera module.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate voltage drop for your specific cable and power requirements; use thicker cable for higher power or longer distances.

faqs.question: What EMC considerations apply to camera systems?
faqs.answer: Camera systems must meet automotive EMC requirements including CISPR 25 for emissions and ISO 11452 for immunity. Key design considerations include proper shielding termination, filtered power entry, and controlled signal transitions. Use coaxial cable with proper shielding and implement common-mode chokes where appropriate. Follow OEM-specific EMC requirements which may be more stringent than industry standards.
faqs.decisionGuide: Follow automotive EMC design guidelines and test early in development; engage EMC experts for complex systems.

faqs.question: How do I implement functional safety in camera systems?
faqs.answer: Functional safety implementation requires systematic approach including safety analysis, diagnostic coverage, and fault handling. Use ASIL-capable SerDes devices with built-in diagnostics. Implement end-to-end safety mechanisms including CRC on video data, timeout monitoring, and redundant paths where required. Follow ISO 26262 development process and document safety case.
faqs.decisionGuide: Engage functional safety experts early; use Jisemi safety documentation and follow ISO 26262 process.

faqs.question: What diagnostic capabilities are available for production testing?
faqs.answer: Jisemi SerDes devices provide comprehensive diagnostics including cable fault detection (open/short), link quality monitoring, eye diagram measurement, and BIST. These features enable automated production testing to verify cable integrity and device functionality. Implement test sequences that verify all diagnostic features and log results for quality tracking.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement comprehensive diagnostic testing in production; use Jisemi provided test routines as reference.

solutions.title: Automotive Gateway and Zonal E/E Architecture

At the core of this solution is a carefully selected portfolio of jisemi components optimized for the target application. The design leverages the latest advances in power management, signal processing, and thermal management to achieve industry-leading efficiency and performance metrics.

Key technical features include high-efficiency power conversion, precise control algorithms, and comprehensive protection mechanisms. Advanced monitoring and diagnostic capabilities enable predictive maintenance and optimize system uptime.

faeInsight.summary: Zonal E/E architecture design requires careful planning of network topology, bandwidth allocation, and safety mechanisms. Key success factors include proper PHY selection, network configuration, and redundancy implementation.
faeInsight.decisionLogic: Select PHY speed based on bandwidth requirements of connected zones. Higher bandwidth zones require multi-gigabit PHYs.
faeInsight.solutionFramework: Design network topology based on data flow requirements, implement TSN for deterministic traffic, validate with network simulation.
keyPoints: Plan network topology based on data flow patterns
Design redundant paths for safety-critical functions
Validate bandwidth allocation with worst-case analysis
commonIssues: Poor network topology causing excessive latency
Inadequate EMC design for high-speed signals
bestPractices: Use network simulation tools for architecture validation

faeInsights.title: Senior FAE - Automotive Networking
faeInsights.content: Zonal E/E architecture represents a paradigm shift in automotive networking. Successful implementation requires careful bandwidth planning and network topology design. I recommend starting with a thorough analysis of data flows between zones and domains. Use TSN features to guarantee bandwidth for safety-critical traffic. For multi-gigabit links, pay special attention to signal integrity and PCB layout - follow Jisemi's layout guidelines closely. Redundancy is critical for safety; implement dual paths for autonomous driving functions. Network simulation tools are invaluable for validating architecture before hardware implementation. Monitor network utilization during testing to ensure headroom for future expansion.
keyTakeaways: Thorough bandwidth planning is essential for zonal architecture success
TSN enables deterministic communication for safety-critical functions
Redundancy is mandatory for autonomous driving systems
Network simulation validates architecture before hardware build
keyPoints: Analyze data flows between zones and domains
commonIssues: Signal integrity issues at high speeds
bestPractices: Use network simulation for architecture validation

steps: Analyze data flow requirements between zones
Design network topology with appropriate redundancy
Select PHY speeds based on bandwidth requirements
Implement TSN configuration for deterministic traffic

faqs.question: What is zonal E/E architecture?
faqs.answer: Zonal E/E architecture organizes vehicle electronics into physical zones (front, rear, left, right) with zonal controllers managing local sensors and actuators. High-speed Ethernet connects zones to central computers. This architecture reduces wiring complexity, enables ECU consolidation, and supports software-defined vehicle functionality.
faqs.decisionGuide: Consider zonal architecture for new vehicle platforms; benefits increase with platform complexity and autonomous driving features.

faqs.question: How much bandwidth is needed for automotive backbone?
faqs.answer: Backbone bandwidth requirements depend on vehicle features. Entry-level vehicles may require 1 Gbps, while premium vehicles with autonomous driving require 10 Gbps or more. Calculate bandwidth based on camera streams, sensor data, infotainment content, and diagnostic traffic. Include 50% headroom for future expansion.
faqs.decisionGuide: Calculate current requirements and add 50% margin; select next standard speed (1/2.5/5/10 Gbps) above calculated requirement.

faqs.question: What is Time-Sensitive Networking (TSN) and why is it important?
faqs.answer: TSN is a set of IEEE standards (802.1AS, 802.1Qbv, etc.) that enable deterministic communication over Ethernet. TSN provides guaranteed latency and bandwidth for critical traffic, essential for safety-critical automotive functions. Jisemi PHYs support TSN features including precise time synchronization and traffic shaping.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement TSN for any safety-critical or time-sensitive traffic; required for autonomous driving and ADAS.

faqs.question: How do I implement redundancy in automotive Ethernet networks?
faqs.answer: Redundancy can be implemented using ring topologies with RSTP/MRP protocols or parallel redundant paths with PRP. For safety-critical functions, implement dual independent networks. Jisemi PHYs support various redundancy protocols. Design redundancy to ensure no single point of failure for autonomous driving functions.
faqs.decisionGuide: Use ring redundancy for cost-sensitive applications, dual networks for safety-critical autonomous driving functions.

faqs.question: What cybersecurity features are needed for automotive Ethernet?
faqs.answer: Automotive Ethernet requires comprehensive cybersecurity including MACsec for link-layer encryption, firewalling between domains, intrusion detection, and secure boot. Implement defense-in-depth with multiple security layers. Follow ISO/SAE 21434 cybersecurity standard and UN R155/R156 regulations.
faqs.decisionGuide: Implement defense-in-depth security; engage cybersecurity experts early in design process.

faqs.question: What types of solutions does Jisemi offer?
faqs.answer: Jisemi offers comprehensive connectivity solutions for automotive and industrial applications including ADAS camera networks, automotive gateway systems, industrial automation networks, and high-speed interface solutions. All solutions are built with Jisemi's high-performance PHYs, SerDes, and interface ICs with appropriate automotive or industrial qualification.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our solution portfolio to find designs matching your application requirements, or contact our FAE team for custom solution recommendations.

faqs.question: How can I get support for implementing Jisemi solutions?
faqs.answer: LiTong provides comprehensive technical support for Jisemi solutions including reference designs, application notes, schematic review, and on-site support. Our FAE team has extensive experience with automotive and industrial networking applications and can assist with design optimization, troubleshooting, and production scaling.
faqs.decisionGuide: Contact our FAE team early in your design cycle for optimal support and to leverage our reference designs.

faqs.question: Are Jisemi solutions scalable for different application sizes?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jisemi solutions are designed with modularity and scalability in mind. Our reference designs can be adapted from simple single-camera systems to complex multi-zone automotive networks. The modular approach allows you to start with a basic configuration and expand as your requirements grow.
faqs.decisionGuide: Start with our reference designs and scale based on your camera count, bandwidth requirements, and system complexity.

faqs.question: What industries are Jisemi solutions designed for?
faqs.answer: Jisemi solutions are optimized for automotive electronics (ADAS, autonomous driving, in-vehicle networking), industrial automation (factory automation, robotics), and telecommunications applications. Our solutions address the specific requirements of each industry including temperature ranges, reliability standards, EMC compliance, and safety certifications.
faqs.decisionGuide: Select solutions based on your industry's specific requirements for temperature, reliability, and certification standards.

faqs.question: Can Jisemi solutions be customized for specific requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes, Jisemi solutions can be customized to meet specific application requirements. Customization options include modifying channel counts, adjusting data rates, adding redundancy, and integrating additional features. For high-volume applications, Jisemi can develop custom variants optimized for your specific needs.
faqs.decisionGuide: For volume applications, discuss customization early in the design cycle to optimize cost and performance.
